Empires & Puzzles Rayne Review: The Contagious Assassin

Rayne isn't just a sniper; she's patient zero. With a contagious poison that spreads like gossip in a small town, she turns a single target into a team-wide catastrophe. We break down her mechanics, the critical speed buff that saved her, and why she's still a threat.
Rayne: The Fast-Spreading Nightmare of the Raven Court

Rayne is a 5-star Legendary Dark hero from the Clash of Knights Alliance Quest. Originally released as a somewhat sluggish Average speed hero, she received a critical lifeline in the December 2023 balance update that upgraded her to Fast mana speed. She is a tactical sniper whose primary gimmick isn't just the initial impact, but the lingering, spreading doom she leaves behind. She represents the "Patient Zero" archetype in Empires & Puzzles—hit one, infect them all. While she hails from the Raven family, making her immune to cleansing if you stack her family members, she stands alone perfectly fine as a purveyor of biological warfare.

Rayne's design is a striking blend of high-fantasy knight and plague doctor chic. Clad in ornate, silver-blue armor with purple plumage, she looks like a Valkyrie who decided to specialize in toxicology. The visual contrast between the pristine armor and the "Plague Blade" skill name implies a beautiful but deadly efficiency. It is a nice change of pace to see a poison-themed hero who isn't dripping in green slime or covered in boils; Rayne delivers her toxins with class and impeccable posture.
Rayne's stats, while impressive upon her release, now sit in the "respectable but aging" category.
Her base attack is solid, and with Limit Breaks, it breaks the 1000 threshold, which is crucial because her poison damage scales with her attack stat.
These are average by modern standards. She isn't a glass cannon, but she certainly isn't a tank.

Compared to the newest Construct or Astral Elf heroes who boast base stats nearing her Limit Broken stats, she falls behind in raw survivability. However, her Fighter class gives her that annoying revive chance which often compensates for the lower HP pool.
Deals 400% damage to a single target. This is a decent snipe, capable of softening up a target or finishing off a wounded one.
The target receives 1300 Poison damage over 5 turns. This effect spreads to nearby enemies at the start of each turn.

The magic here is the "contagious" tag. If the target survives the initial hit, they become a carrier. Every turn, the poison jumps to their neighbors. If left unchecked, Rayne effectively casts a heavy DoT (Damage over Time) on the entire enemy team from a single hit. The update to
was a game-changer, allowing her to apply this pressure much earlier in the match, often before the enemy can set up immunity or cleanses.

the target to survive the initial impact.
Let's break down the lethality of the Plague Blade.
Fast. Requires 8 tiles to charge, or 7 with a level 29 Mana Troop (or equivalent bonuses).
400%. With an LB2 Attack of ~1005, against a standard defense (approx. 1000), this hits for roughly 600-700 damage.
If the poison spreads to all 5 enemies and runs its full course (unlikely, but theoretically), that is 1300 x 5 = 6500 damage from a single special skill.
Usually, it spreads to 2-3 heroes, dealing ~2000-3000 total damage across the team over a few turns.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Rayne benefits significantly from her family ties and modern Aether powers.
The Raven Family bonus is exceptionally strong for Rayne. With just two unique Raven heroes, you get a 35% chance to make the special skill cast
. If you have three, it becomes a 50% chance.
An uncleansable 1300 damage poison is a death sentence. This makes pairing her with other Ravens (like Quintin or Lewena) highly incentivized.
Rayne does not have innate passives outside of the Family Bonus mechanics, which is typical for her release era.
Heals for a percentage of normal damage dealt.
This is excellent for Rayne. Since she is a Fighter, she might revive with 1 HP. The Vampire passive on tile damage helps her sustain herself and stay in the fight long enough to fire another plague.
When Rayne launched, she was considered good but slow at Average speed. The "contagious" mechanic was novel, but the meta was speeding up. She was often overshadowed by her family member, Quintin.
The raw 400% snipe is now low for a Fast sniper (many deal 500%+). However, the DoT has been buffed significantly over time (from ~748 to 1300).

The buff to Fast speed saved her from the bench. While she isn't top-tier for defense due to the prevalence of ailment immunity (Hathor, Aramis), she is a stellar offensive weapon. She shines in wars against teams without a cleanser, where her poison inevitably wears down the opposition.
Rayne faces the classic dilemma: hit harder or live longer?

Fighter is arguably the best class for a sniper; reviving and firing a game-winning shot is a common occurrence.
The potential for uncleansable ailments is a hard counter to heavy cleanse teams.
If she kills her target with the direct damage, the poison effect is lost. This is counter-intuitive for a sniper.
Heroes who block ailments (Garnet, Hathor, Vanda) completely neutralize her main threat.
Without the family bonus proc, a simple Rigard or Vivica can wipe away her entire contribution.
Excellent for picking apart teams. Use her to hit a healthy flank, infect the tank and wing, and watch them rot.

your heavy hitters to apply the poison, or target a full-health enemy.
Rayne + 2 Healers + 2 Counter-attackers. Keep the team alive while Rayne's poison slowly eats the enemy alive.
Hathor, Aramis, and Garnet are her nightmares. They prevent the poison entirely. Toon Costume heroes with their passive resistance are also a major issue.

If you pull Rayne early, she is a carry hero. Her poison damage is fixed, meaning it devastates low-level teams regardless of their defense. Focus on keeping her alive and targeting the enemy with the most health to ensure the poison spreads.

Rayne is a solid depth hero for War. She pairs exceptionally well with older Raven heroes if you have them. Do not use Alpha Aethers on her unless you are a huge fan of the contagious mechanic; there are better stats available elsewhere. However, she is a safe bet for a regular Limit Break (85) to keep her relevant.
The community's relationship with Rayne has been a rollercoaster.
"Rayne is durvább (tougher) than I thought... poison every turn on neighbors!"
"It's really hard to spread it when you can't kill the enemy with snipe... It would make so much more sense if the poison would spread when the target dies from it!"
"Seriously?! Is this a hero to charge at fast speed?! She's basically a Lady Locke on steroids now."
"I’m looking forward to using her with Lewena and Franz for that sweet 50% chance to be uncleansable."

Rayne is the gift that keeps on giving... herpes. Or plague. Whatever it is, she ensures that sharing is caring on the battlefield. While her stats show her age, the mechanic of spreading poison remains one of the most satisfying ways to win a match. She requires a bit of thinking—don't just tap the low HP enemy—but the reward is watching five health bars turn green and tick down to zero simultaneously.
A lethal tactical option that aged like fine wine (or perhaps fine poison) thanks to a speed buff. Keep her off the bench and in your war bands.
A deep dive into Rayne, the 5-star Dark hero from the Raven family in Empires & Puzzles. We analyze her stats, contagious poison mechanics, and viability in the current meta.
